section.title: Opening Procedures
section.responsibility: Whoever is opening. Arrive by 8:30 AM.
section.step: Unlock front and back doors.
Turn on lights and HVAC.
Start the hot lather machine.
Wipe down all stations, mirrors, and chairs.
Sweep the floor.
Check station supply levels: blades, neck strips, disinfectant, capes, towels.
Boot the point-of-sale tablet and verify the internet connection.
Start the shop playlist for the day.
Unlock the front door at 9:00 AM sharp.

section.title: Closing Procedures
section.step: Last client finished by 6:45 PM weekdays, 1:45 PM Sundays.
Sweep and mop all floors.
Wipe down all stations and mirrors.
Clean and oil all clippers.
Empty and refill the disinfectant jars for the next day.
Take out the trash.
Run the point-of-sale end-of-day report and confirm totals.
Lock the retail product display case.
Turn off HVAC, lights, and the hot lather machine.
Lock the back door, then the front door.
Set the alarm system before leaving.

section.title: Sanitation Standards
section.rule: Disinfect every tool that touches a client between each client.
Fresh neck strip and a clean cape for every person in the chair.
Disinfectant jars refreshed daily, never reused across days.
Floors swept between clients during busy stretches, not just at close.
section.complianceNote: Michigan licensing requires it, clients expect it, and it reflects who we are.

section.title: Equipment Maintenance Log Rules
section.rule: Log clipper blade sharpening or replacement on the maintenance board.
One card per open issue: chair upholstery, plumbing, signage, until done.
section.reorderPolicy: Reorder station consumables when stock hits the reorder point. James places supplier orders himself.

section.title: Client Standards
section.rule: Greet every person who walks in, even mid-cut.
Offer water or coffee to anyone waiting.
If a barber is running behind, give an honest wait time. Do not guess low.
No phones during a cut unless it is an emergency.
Treat every client like a returning client.
